pr.title: diff_settings: Pick up DEVKITPRO/PPC from env
pr.author: r-burns

summary: Updated diff_settings.py so the objdump path respects both DEVKITPRO and DEVKITPPC environment variables, while preserving the previous default devkit path behavior when those variables are unset.
what_changed: Replaced direct indexing of os.environ['DEVKITPRO'] with os.environ.get('DEVKITPRO', '/opt/devkitpro'), avoiding a required DEVKITPRO environment variable.
Added DEVKITPPC lookup via os.environ.get('DEVKITPPC', f'{devkitpro}/devkitPPC').
Changed config['objdump_executable'] to derive from devkitppc rather than hard-coding the devkitPPC subdirectory under DEVKITPRO.
Kept the objdump binary name and get_exe_suffix() behavior unchanged.
smart_moves: Matched Makefile workflow expectations by allowing DEVKITPPC to drive the PowerPC toolchain location.
Preserved old behavior with defaults: DEVKITPRO defaults to /opt/devkitpro and DEVKITPPC defaults to $DEVKITPRO/devkitPPC.
Made local developer setups more flexible without changing map_format, build_dir, or other diff configuration.
decomp_lessons: Diff tooling should honor the same environment variables as the build system where possible, so developers do not need separate configuration for matching and diffing.
When adding environment-variable support, use fallbacks that preserve established defaults to avoid breaking existing setups.
Avoid mandatory os.environ indexing for optional local configuration; os.environ.get with a stable default is safer for shared scripts.
naming_conventions: Environment variable names use DEVKITPRO and DEVKITPPC, matching devkitPro/devkitPPC ecosystem conventions.
Local Python variables mirror the environment variable names in lowercase: devkitpro and devkitppc.
The configured executable remains config['objdump_executable'] and points to powerpc-eabi-objdump plus get_exe_suffix().
assembly_or_matching_tactics: No assembly matching tactic changed directly.
This improves diff workflow reliability by making the objdump executable path configurable through environment variables.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
The PR body states that DEVKITPPC is already used in the Makefile and that the change works better for the author's workflow.
The PR body explicitly notes that the script defaults to the same paths as before when environment variables are not specified.
follow_up_queries: Find other tooling scripts that hard-code DEVKITPRO or devkitPPC paths.
Check whether Makefile and diff_settings.py remain aligned on DEVKITPPC handling.
Search for os.environ['DEVKITPRO'] usages that should use os.environ.get with defaults.
